channel.title: Linux on Personal Wiki
channel.description: Recent content in Linux on Personal Wiki

item.title: Lookup
item.description:
Test your enumeration skills on this boot-to-root machine.

This is an easy machine, lets start of by scanning the machine using nmap

Reconnaissance

> nmap [ip addr]

We can see that it has 2 ports open, now lets take a closer look at

> nmap [ip addr] -p 22,80 -A

After a little bit of investigation you can find that there is no known vulnerabilities which target OpenSSH 8.2p1 and Apache 2.4.41 so we might wanna take a look at the site itself

item.title: HF 2019-Linux
item.description:
We are going to try to break into HF 2019-Linux machine which can be downloaded here

Let start off by basic scanning using nmap

Reconnaissance

> nmap [ip addr]

We can see that there is 4 ports available in this machine, now lets take a closer look at the ports

> nmap [ip addr] -p 21,22,80,10000 -A

We can see that there are a lot of files in the ftp server available for us, in order to download all the files available in the server we can use wget

item.title: Sunset Walkthrough - Vulnhub
item.description:
Sunset is crafted by the Whitecr0wz. This blog provides information on how to exploit the underlaying vulnerabilities in sunset machine

You can also easily download sunset through vulnhub

First we need to take a look at the available ports on the machine by port scanning using nmap

Reconnaissance

> nmap [ip addr]

This show that the machine has 2 ports open, lets scan for more info

> nmap [ip addr] -p 21,22 -A

item.title: Why Linux
item.description:
Linux is the foundation of almost everything interesting in computing — servers, Android, embedded systems, and most of the open source world runs on it.

Why Linux

After switching from Windows I noticed a few things immediately. The terminal is actually useful. Software installation is one command. The system does what you tell it to do rather than the other way around.

Where to start

If you have never used Linux before, start with a distribution that has good documentation and a large community. Arch Linux is not the place to start — but the ArchWiki is useful regardless of which distribution you use.
